title: Lane 1 season-two station clarity
summary: Prepare the station and season surfaces for a broader beta runway without adding a larger planner or new content lane work.

execution_order.goal: Scope the smallest station/season clarity pass for post-Source-to-Shore next-step readability.
execution_order.why_now: The station can now house Source to Shore cleanly, so it needs a calm runway into broader beta growth.

execution_order.goal: Implement the scoped station clarity pass using existing station/session surfaces.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 1 should make the next chapter easier to find before route or content breadth expands.

execution_order.goal: Review station clarity for 256x160 fit, no dashboard drift, and stable Source to Shore closure.
execution_order.why_now: Station structure should stay quiet before more beta work starts pointing at it.

completion_gate: Station next-step readability improves after filed Source to Shore.
Source to Shore stays a clean three-beat endpoint unless separately scoped.
No planner, dashboard, season-three shell, content pack, route catalog change, or save schema change lands.
Focused station/runtime tests and build pass.
guardrails: Use existing surfaces and helper seams.
Do not absorb lane 2 content, lane 3 geometry, or lane 4 route authoring.
post_packet_path: If clean, proceed to lane-1 travel and proof hardening.
If blocked, open a lane-1-local blocker rather than gating other lanes.

scout_handoff_487.finding: After Source to Shore is fully filed, the station expedition subtitle can still say High Pass opens the next field season while the expedition card is SOURCE TO SHORE / FILED.
implementation_contract: Centralize filed Source to Shore terminal copy in src/engine/source-to-shore-state.ts.
Use that copy in the filed Dune Catch state without changing the three-beat flow.
Update src/engine/field-season-wrap.ts so filed Source to Shore archive subtitles do not fall through to the High Pass expedition subtitle.
Keep src/engine/field-station-routes-page.ts unchanged unless a focused proof shows a rendering issue.
verification_plan: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|subtitle|filed arc"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Source to Shore station|Dune Catch|season capstone"
scout_handoff_487.guardrail: No planner, dashboard, new station page, route catalog entry, save schema change, content pack, geometry, season-three shell, or fourth Source to Shore beat.

main_implementation_487.summary: Centralized filed Source to Shore terminal copy and used it for filed archive subtitles so SEASON -> EXPEDITION no longer says High Pass opens the next field season after Dune Catch is filed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|subtitle|filed arc"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Source to Shore station|Dune Catch|season capstone"
main_implementation_487.guardrail: Kept Source to Shore at exactly three beats with no route id, catalog, save, station page, planner, dashboard, content, geometry, or layout change.

notes: Filed Source to Shore subtitles now resolve as current-field-arc closure instead of the High Pass launch subtitle.
The dedicated three-beat SOURCE TO SHORE board remains intact.
No planner, dashboard, new station page, route catalog, save, content, geometry, or rendering layout change landed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|subtitle|filed arc"
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Source to Shore station|Dune Catch|season capstone"
